name: The Security National Bank Of Sioux City, Iowa
description_short: Community bank headquartered in Sioux City, Iowa with 13 branch locations across Iowa and South Dakota, offering retail banking, mortgages, and financial education tools.
description_long: Security National Bank is a local community bank based in Sioux City, Iowa with a history of serving the region. The bank operates 13 physical branch locations across Iowa and South Dakota, including locations in Sioux City, Orange City, Sheldon, Moville, Akron, Mapleton, Lawton, Dakota Dunes, and Sioux Falls. Their positioning emphasizes personal service and local decision-making as differentiators from larger national banks. The bank offers a comprehensive range of retail banking services including checking and savings accounts, mortgages, online and mobile banking, and financial planning tools. Specific digital tools include a free mortgage calculator, live credit score access through their mobile app, personalized financial roadmap generation based on age and income, and an interactive Financial Learning Center. They also provide fraud detection services, as evidenced by customer testimonials describing proactive fraud monitoring. Mortgage lending is a significant service line with a dedicated team accessible at 712-277-6785. Security National Bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on live customer service and accessibility. Multiple customer reviews highlight the ability to speak directly with a live person without navigating phone menus or button systems. The bank maintains extended branch hours, including Saturday and Sunday availability at some locations (notably the Marketplace location inside HyVee operates Sunday 10A-2P). Their recent marketing emphasizes personalization through financial education and custom financial planning rather than transactional relationships. The bank operates within the standard limitations of regional community banks: service area is geographically limited to Iowa and South Dakota, technology infrastructure appears dated (website shows browser compatibility warnings), and product offerings appear more limited compared to national banks. Customer satisfaction appears generally positive based on testimonials, though the sample size is small. The bank's emphasis on personal relationships and local presence suggests strongest appeal to customers prioritizing service quality and community connection over digital-first banking or maximum product variety.
services: Mortgage origination and lending (home purchase and refinance)
Mobile banking application with credit score access
pros: Live customer service representatives available without phone menu navigation, per customer testimonials
Mobile app provides real-time credit score access to customers
Personalized financial roadmap generation tool available at no cost
Free interactive Financial Learning Center for financial education
Proactive fraud detection and monitoring on customer accounts
Extended branch hours including Saturday and Sunday availability at select locations
Multiple convenient branch locations across Iowa and South Dakota with both lobby and drive-up services
cons: Website displays outdated browser compatibility warnings suggesting aging technology infrastructure
Service area limited to Iowa and South Dakota only, not available nationally
Limited product information on website; mortgage contact required for details on PMI, HOA, and specific loan terms
Smaller regional bank may lack some product variety or rates competitive with larger national banks
No mention of business banking, investment services, or wealth management products on primary website
best_for: Iowa and South Dakota residents prioritizing personal service and local community banking relationships
Homebuyers in the bank's service area seeking mortgage lending with direct lender communication
Customers seeking banks with extended hours and multiple physical branch locations for convenience
Consumers valuing fraud detection and proactive account monitoring services
diagnosis: Security National Bank is best suited for Iowa and South Dakota residents who value personal relationships, local decision-making, and direct access to bank representatives over digital-convenience features. The main caveat is geographic limitation to their service area and apparent technology infrastructure limitations that may concern digitally-native customers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community bank headquartered in Sioux City, Iowa with 13 branch locations across Iowa and South Dakota, offering retail banking and mortgages.
fdic_official_name: The Security National Bank of Sioux City, Iowa
